full_text:
Storm and rain warning in Rajasthan for two days:Heatwave in 3 districts; temperature crosses 40°C in Barmer; guidelines issued
- Copy link
Heatwave continued to affect the western districts of Rajasthan on Wednesday. Loo (hot winds) blew in the afternoon in the areas of Jaisalmer, Barmer, and Balotra. The maximum temperature here was recorded between 39 and 41 degrees. Considering the intense heat in the coming days, the Health Department has issued guidelines to prevent heatwave.
Meanwhile, light clouds covered the sky in the areas of Ganganagar and Hanumangarh in the afternoon, leading to a slight drop in temperature here. The Meteorological Center Jaipur has issued a forecast stating that the heatwave spell will end from Thursday and the state's weather will change from March 14. A yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain has been issued for 8 districts on March 14.
Highest temperature in Barmer Today, the highest maximum temperature of 40.8 degrees was recorded in Barmer in the state. With maximum temperatures of 39.1 degrees in Jaisalmer and 39.2 degrees in Phalodi, these areas experienced heatwave conditions during the day. Barmer has been experiencing heatwave conditions for 3 consecutive days.
Major cities temperature in the state (Temperature recorded on 11 March in degrees Celsius)
Apart from these districts, Vanasthali (Tonk) recorded 38.1 degrees, Chittorgarh 38.6 degrees, Jodhpur and Churu 38.1 degrees, Bhilwara, Udaipur and Sirohi 37.2 degrees, Dausa 37.3 degrees, Kota and Jaipur 37 degrees. All these cities experienced scorching heat during the day. Here, the maximum temperature was 5 to 7 degrees above normal.
Health Department issued guidelines for heatwave Considering the onset of the heatwave and its potential to intensify in the coming days, the Health Department has issued guidelines. The department has issued instructions to maintain an adequate stock of medicines in every government hospital.
It has been advised to arrange coolers-fans, ACs, drinking water, and shade to protect patients and their accompanying persons from the heat. Additionally, it has been instructed to reserve 10 beds in district-level hospitals, 3 to 6 beds at Community Health Centers (CHCs), and 1 bed at Primary Health Centers (PHCs).
Slight relief from heatwave from tomorrow The effect of the Western Disturbance, which became active in North India, was seen on Wednesday at the northern tip of Rajasthan. Light clouds covered the areas of Sriganganagar, Bikaner, and Hanumangarh in the afternoon.
This led to a slight drop in the maximum daytime temperatures in these cities. Weather experts have predicted a weakening of westerly winds and relief from the heatwave in the state from March 12. However, there will be no significant change in temperature.
